channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Build your focus stack with these proven tools and gadgets
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. A dozen tabs open. Notifications pinging. I just opened my laptop and unread emails are already calling my name.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: The Price of Bitcoin Surges Amid Expectations of Trump's Pro-Crypto Presidency
item.description: Bitcoin price surges amid expectations of pro-crypto presidency under Donald Trump
item.dc:creator: Alan Suderman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: From backing a ban to being hailed as a savior: Inside Trump's TikTok shift
item.description: During his first term as president, Donald Trump led the effort to ban TikTok, the hugely popular video-sharing site he said posed threats to U.S. national security.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin And Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Live updates: Trump holds victory rally at Capital One Arena, TikTok restores service
item.description: President-elect Trump made the rounds rounds in Washington on Sunday and held a victory rally at Capital One Area just before Inauguration Day. Trump arrived in the nation's capital over the weekend where he is preparing to take a second term in the White House as the Republican trifecta takes form.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: Content creators respond to TikTok's uncertain future in the US
item.description: TikTok content creators who make a living promoting their businesses and personal brands on the app were afraid that they would have to find new ways to engage consumers and make money as the app’s future in the United States remained uncertain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: UPDATE: Trump says he will issue an executive order Monday to get TikTok back after ban
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump said Sunday that he plans to issue an executive order that would give TikTok’s China-based parent company more time to find an approved buyer before the popular video-sharing platform is subject to a permanent U.S.ban.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: How TikTok grew from a fun app for teens into a potential national security threat
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — If it feels like TikTok has been around forever, that's probably because it has, at least if you're measuring via internet time.
item.dc:creator: DAVID HAMILTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nile dials-up AI to simplify network provisioning, operation
item.description: Network-as-a-service startup Nile has added an AI-based tool aimed at helping enterprise customers provision and operate the vendor’s Campus Network-as-a-Service deployments.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Network convergence will drive enterprise 6G wireless strategies
item.description: The traditional separation between Wi-Fi and cellular networks — a cornerstone of enterprise wireless planning — is facing fundamental challenges according to a new 6G vision statement released this week by the World Broadband Association, “It is important that the 6G era brings together cellular, W
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Taiwanese chipmaker TSMC posts 57% surge in profit thanks to the AI boom
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Taiwan computer chip maker TSMC reported Thursday that its profit in the last quarter rose 57%, buoyed by the artificial intelligence boom. The world’s biggest semiconductor manufacturer reported a net profit of 374.7 billion new Taiwan dollars ($11.4 billion), higher than expected.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Fox will stream next month's Super Bowl for free on Tubi
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fox will stream the Super Bowl on Tubi for the first time, giving streamers and cord cutters a chance to watch the big game on Feb. 9 for free. Tubi was acquired by Fox Corporation in 2020. It is a free, ad-supported streaming service.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden's big semiconductor law will ramp up US chip production -- but at a high cost, report finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A sweeping 2022 law, touted by President Joe Biden as a way to revive U.S. manufacturing of semiconductors and reduce the country’s reliance on foreign-made computer chips, will “sharply increase production’’ of semiconductors in the United States.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The death of DEI in tech
item.description: Save me from rich, white men who insist they and their kind are being discriminated against. Tech entrepreneur Elon Musk, majority owner (not the founder) of SpaceX, Tesla, and numerous other leading companies, insists that “DEI [Diversity, equity, and inclusion] is just another word for racism.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cisco offers AI application visibility, access control, threat defense
item.description: Cisco has rolled out a service that promises to protect enterprise AI development projects with visibility, access control, threat defense, and other safeguards.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: What Nokia was thinking when Apple introduced iPhone in 2007
item.description: The whole world was watching when legendary Apple CEO Steve Jobs launched the iconic iPhone in 2007. Mobile was the future. Apple knew it (and rebranded itself to reflect it). And Nokia had a fight on its hands. So, what did Nokia think about the iPhone launch? Now we know.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why you should use Docker and OCI containers
item.description: A book published in 1981, called Nailing Jelly to a Tree, describes software as “nebulous and difficult to get a firm grip on.” That was true in 1981, and it is no less true four decades later.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg will cohost reception with Republican billionaires for Trump inauguration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg is cohosting a reception with billionaire Republican donors next week for Donald Trump’s inauguration, the latest sign of the Facebook founder's embrace of the president-elect.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Biden signs ambitious order to bolster energy resources for AI data centers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Joe Biden on Tuesday signed an ambitious executive order on artificial intelligence that seeks to ensure the infrastructure needed for advanced AI operations, such as large-scale data centers and new clean power facilities, can be built quickly and at scale in the United
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Court lets Tennessee porn site age verification law take effect as Texas law goes to Supreme Court
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A panel of appeals judges has ruled that Tennessee can begin enforcing a law that requires pornographic websites to verify their visitors’ age as the First Amendment debate rises to the U.S. Supreme Court this week for oral arguments over a similar Texas law.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hackers claim massive breach of company that tracks and sells Americans' location data
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals that hackers stole data from Gravy Analytics, a major seller of smartphone location data to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: HPE beats Dell and Supermicro in $1B AI server deal with X
item.description: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has signed a contract exceeding $1 billion to provide AI servers for X, the platform formerly known as Twitter, according to Bloomberg.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New Brazil law restricts use of smartphones in elementary and high schools
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazil's President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva on Monday signed a bill restricting the use of smartphones at school, following a global trend for such limitations. The move will impact students at elementary and high schools across the South American nation starting in February.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE and DIARLEI RODRIGUES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Britain's leader unveils AI strategy, including vow to rip up red tape hindering AI projects
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The British government pledged on Monday to rip up the red tape hindering construction of data centers that underpin artificial intelligence while promoting its “pro-innovation” approach to regulating the technology.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4
